name: Utah DOPL Contractor License Lookup
updateFrequency: The official lookup page displays a current-as-of date; verify at lookup time before relying on freshness assumptions.
licenseTypesIncluded: Utah DOPL contractor license records exposed by the Licensee Lookup & Verification System
B100, R100, E100, H100, E200, E201, P200, P201, and specialty contractor classifications identified by DOPL contracting pages and rules
knownExclusions: Local permits, business registrations, and non-DOPL authorizations are outside this source entry.
Classification and qualifier details must be preserved before normalization.
Utah rules identify asbestos removal as regulated by the Department of Environmental Quality rather than requiring a DOPL contractor license.
rateLimitNotes: No adapter is implemented. Do not automate lookup pages unless access terms and technical controls are reviewed.
publicRecordsNotes: Official lookup source metadata only. Absence from this source is not proof that a license does not exist elsewhere.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: No official bulk download has been confirmed in this phase.
researchNotes: Registry-only source for Utah DOPL contractor lookup. DOPL pages, rules, and classification materials identify statewide general, residential, commercial, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, mechanical, roofing, solar, pool/recreation, underground-utility, sheet-metal, and home-improvement-relevant classifications.
maintainerNotes: Preserve the lookup page's freshness wording and keep asbestos caveats visible if adapter work begins.

researchEvidence.note: Official public source, access shape, and current automation constraints reviewed.
The official DOPL general-contractor page lists B100, R100, E100, P200, P201, E200, and E201 contractor classifications.
The official DOPL specialty-contractor page directs applicants to Utah administrative rules for available specialty contractor classifications and scope of practice.
The official Utah Construction Trades Licensing Act Rule identifies specialty contractor classifications and notes asbestos removal is regulated by the Department of Environmental Quality.
The official DOPL classification conversion chart identifies prior S340 Sheet Metal Contractor coverage and maps it into the current S220 Carpentry and Flooring Contractor classification.

blocker.summary: No stable, documented machine-readable official interface is currently suitable for an offline-tested adapter.
